All States - Wisconsin

Wisconsin Respiratory Therapist License Reciprocity & How to Apply

Start with the only questions that matter for travelers: does your current license already cover Wisconsin, what does endorsement cost, and how quickly can you get cleared to work?

Last verified 2026-02-25 Official board Wisconsin DSPS — Respiratory Care Practitioner mid tier
No Interstate Compact Decision point for travel-ready licensing

How to Transfer Your Respiratory Therapist License to Wisconsin

There is no active interstate compact covering respiratory therapist practice in Wisconsin. To work here, plan on a full endorsement or transfer application through Wisconsin DSPS — Respiratory Care Practitioner.

Good news: the exact cost, documentation, and board path are laid out below so there is no guesswork.

Before You Start

  1. Open the official board portal before gathering documents.
  2. Budget the endorsement fee and expected processing window below.
  3. Ask about the temporary or provisional path if you need to start sooner.
Endorsement Fee $100 State-level transfer cost
Processing Time 4 to 6 Weeks Best estimate from current board data
Fingerprinting Required Background screening expectation
Temporary License Available Useful if you need a faster start date
See Endorsement Steps Official Board Portal
Steps Quick Facts Documents Board Renewal FAQ

Endorsement Application Steps

Work through these in order. This sequence is the fastest way to move from out-of-state license to a board-cleared application in Wisconsin.

  1. Complete Education Requirements

    Complete a CoARC-accredited respiratory care program and any required clinical practicum or fieldwork.

  2. Pass the NBRC

    Successfully pass the required NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam.

  3. Submit Application and Fees

    Complete the state application, undergo a background check if required, and submit the necessary licensing fees.

Quick Facts

Total fee
$100
Processing time
4 to 6 Weeks
Renewal fee
$50
Renewal cycle
Every 2 years

Required Documents

  • Official Transcripts: Sent directly from your educational institution.
  • Exam Score Report: Official verification of passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam.

Board Contact

Board
Wisconsin DSPS — Respiratory Care Practitioner
Department
Wisconsin Department of Safety and Professional Services (DSPS)
Phone
(608) 266-2112
Email
See board website
Visit Official Board Portal

Renewal

Renewal fee is $50. Renewal cycle: Every 2 years.

Wisconsin Respiratory Therapist Reciprocity FAQ

How much does a Wisconsin respiratory therapist license cost?

The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

Is national certification required?

Yes, passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

Does Wisconsin have reciprocity for respiratory therapists?

No interstate compact currently covers respiratory therapist practice in Wisconsin. To work in Wisconsin, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

How long does it take to transfer my respiratory therapist license to Wisconsin?

Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

Can I start working in Wisconsin while my endorsement application is processing?

A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.
